claim: Hooks are configured in a non-canonical settings file name, so Claude Code won't load them.
symptom: PreToolUse/Stop/SubagentStop gates never execute; deep-review and script-run safeguards silently inactive.
trigger: Any session relying on hooks in .claude/settings (2).json without merging into .claude/settings.json.

evidence.why_it_matters: Claude Code settings are read from .claude/settings.json; a renamed copy won't be loaded, so hooks are inert.

fix_sketch: Move hook configuration into .claude/settings.json or rename the file to settings.json.
test_gap: No automated check ensures hooks are loaded from the canonical settings file.

claim: The python-run gate can be bypassed via interpreter flags or versioned executables.
symptom: Unreviewed scripts can run by invoking `python -u script.py` or `python3.11 script.py`.
trigger: Any Bash command where python flags appear before the script or the interpreter is versioned (python3.X).

evidence.snippet:
m = re.search(r"\bpython(?:3)?(?:\.exe)?\s+(.+)", cmd)
rest = m.group(1).strip()
if rest.startswith("-c") or rest.startswith("-m") or rest.startswith("--") or rest.startswith("-h"): return None
first = rest.split()[0].strip("\"'")
if not first.endswith(".py"): return None
evidence.why_it_matters: Flags before the script make `first` a flag, and python3.11 does not match the regex, so the gate returns None and allows execution.

fix_sketch: Use shlex.split to parse, skip interpreter flags until the first non-flag token, and accept python3.X interpreter names.
test_gap: No tests cover flag-prefixed invocations or python3.X executables.

claim: The gate does not enforce the deep-script-review recency/modified-since requirement.
symptom: Stale PASS reviews allow running modified scripts without re-review.
trigger: A script changes after a PASS review or a review older than 30 days.

evidence.snippet: The review was written within the last 30 days (re-review if older or script modified since)
evidence.why_it_matters: The documented gate requires freshness checks, but the hook only checks PASS marker.

evidence.snippet:
ok, reason = review_passes(review)
if not ok: ...
return 0
evidence.why_it_matters: No timestamp or script-modified-since checks are performed.

fix_sketch: Compare review mtime and script mtime; block if review older than 30 days or script newer than review.
test_gap: No tests for stale review enforcement.

claim: The stop hook does not enforce all sections required by deep-pr-review SKILL.md.
symptom: A REVIEW_REPORT.md missing Base branch / Files reviewed / Confirmed findings can still pass the stop gate.
trigger: Deep review report missing those sections.

evidence.snippet: REVIEW_REPORT.md must contain sections: Scope reviewed, Base branch, Diff source, Files reviewed, Commands run, Subagents used, Confirmed findings, Verified-clean, Dropped candidate findings, Residual risk
evidence.why_it_matters: Skill mandates sections that are not enforced by the stop hook.

evidence.snippet: REQUIRED_SECTIONS = ["Scope reviewed", "Diff source", "Commands run", "Subagents used", "Verified-clean", "Dropped candidate findings", "Residual risk"]
evidence.why_it_matters: Base branch, Files reviewed, Confirmed findings are omitted, weakening the gate.

fix_sketch: Add the missing sections to REQUIRED_SECTIONS to match SKILL.md.
test_gap: No tests ensure section list matches the skill spec.

claim: New gate hooks were introduced without tests to prevent regressions in allow/block logic.
symptom: Future changes could silently break gating behavior.
trigger: Any refactor of hook logic without tests.

evidence.snippet: Only new files under .claude/hooks were added; no tests were added in the diff.
evidence.why_it_matters: Critical gating logic lacks regression coverage.

fix_sketch: Add unit tests for parse/allow/block cases and stop-hook section enforcement.
